sentence_reviews.text: Competing learners optimize opposing goals, as in generator–discriminator or adversarial robustness games.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Goodfellow et al. GAN abstract and method describe generator/discriminator adversarial training; AIMA game-theory/adversarial-search chapters cover opposing-agent optimization.
sentence_reviews.notes: Generator-discriminator minimax is directly supported. Adversarial robustness games are plausible but not directly covered by the cited GAN source.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Adversarial minimax objectives is to connect Generative modeling, Game theory & decision theory to downstream capabilities including Adversarial examples & adversarial training, Generative adversarial networks.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Goodfellow et al. GAN paper, minimax generator-discriminator setup; AIMA chapters on adversarial search, games, and decisions.
sentence_reviews.notes: GAN and game-theory connections are supported, but adversarial examples/training are not directly evidenced by the packet sources.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Adversarial examples & adversarial training
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA adversarial-search/game material; no packet source specifically on adversarial examples or adversarial training was cited.
sentence_reviews.notes: The relation is plausible for robust optimization, but the cited packet evidence is too broad to verify the adversarial-training example.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Goodfellow et al. GAN paper, minimax game between generator and discriminator.
sentence_reviews.notes: The GAN source directly supports adversarial minimax objectives as the basis of GANs.

sentence_reviews.text: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Generative modeling, Game theory & decision theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Goodfellow et al. GAN paper on adversarial training objective; AIMA game/decision theory chapters.
sentence_reviews.notes: The dependencies are supported, but the surrogate-objective failure mode is generic and not directly established by these citations.

sentence_reviews.text: Adversarial minimax objectives uses Generative modeling as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Goodfellow et al. GAN paper, generator learns to model data distribution through adversarial training.
sentence_reviews.notes: The cited GAN evidence directly supports the generative-modeling dependency for this objective family.

sentence_reviews.text: Adversarial minimax objectives uses Game theory & decision theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Goodfellow et al. GAN paper frames training as a minimax game; AIMA game-theory and adversarial-search chapters.
sentence_reviews.notes: The minimax-game framing directly supports this dependency.

sentence_reviews.text: Adversarial minimax objectives uses Optimization theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Goodfellow et al. GAN paper, minimax objective and training procedure.
sentence_reviews.notes: The minimax objective directly requires optimization.

edge_reviews.notes: GANs directly support the generative-learning edge and core strength.
The minimax game framing directly supports a core game/decision-theory dependency.
The cited objective is optimized during training; common strength is defensible relative to the core generative/game dependencies.
